description: Letter from Shelomo b. Eliyyahu to the merchant Abū l-Fakhr. In Judaeo-Arabic. Abū l-Fakhr had asked Shelomo how much he should pay him every day, evidently for working as an assistant in his shop. Shelomo responds that he is only asking for the standard rate that the boys (ṣibyān, ghilmān) in the shops (dakākīn) receive, and he defers to Abū l-Fakhr to pay what is fair.
